title: pseudognaphalium helleri subsp. micradenium
intro: Pseudognaphalium helleri subsp. micradenium, also known as Heller's cudweed, is a plant species native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can aid in wound healing and reducing pain. The health benefits of Pseudognaphalium helleri subsp. micradenium include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and poultices, and its essential oils can be extracted for use in aromatherapy and topical treatments.
